course_description.overview: Pyrenean road sportive starting and finishing in Camprodon through Vall del Bac, featuring climbs over iconic passes on paved roads with stunning mountain scenery and picturesque villages.

key_segments.name: Coll de Santigosa
key_segments.description: Early climb to 1,054 m to warm up the legs.

key_segments.name: Coll de Capsacosta
key_segments.description: Subsequent pass at 890 m through mountainous terrain.

key_segments.name: Port de Rocabruna
key_segments.description: Climb to 1,090 m after passing through Oix and Beget.

key_segments.name: Vallter 2000
key_segments.description: Legendary first-category mountain pass from Volta a Catalunya, tackled only on the longest 144 km route.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Warrior-level challenge with 3,600 m elevation gain over 144 km, including technical sequences, hairpins, and unforgiving climbs starting from 950 m altitude; not forgiving of pacing errors.

logistics.getting_there: Start/finish at Llandríus Sports Center or Camp de futbol Camprodon, Carretera de Molló; accessible by road in Girona province, Catalonia.
logistics.accommodation: Camprodon offers village accommodations; nearby areas in Pyrenees.
logistics.registration_details: Early bird €65 until June 3, 2026; €70 until June 21, 2026; bib pickup Saturday June 27 5-8 PM or Sunday 6:45-8 AM; includes GOBIK jersey, timing, medal, refreshments, pasta party.
logistics.bike_logistics: Certified helmet mandatory; bag drop, showers, mechanical assistance available.
logistics.local_considerations: Pyrenean summer weather with sun and potential heat; post-race sangria, beer, and pasta party; no refunds.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic Pyrenean road sportive with brutal climbs and stunning valleys, SCOTT-sponsored gem for endurance cyclists.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong climbers and gran fondo enthusiasts seeking high-altitude challenges in beautiful Catalonia; suitable for various fitness levels with 78/122/144 km options.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the iconic Vallter 2000 climb, quality inclusions, and value at €65-70.
pro_tips: Train for early climbs and Vallter 2000; pace wisely to beat 4 PM cut-off.
Register early for best price; wear the provided GOBIK jersey for services.
Enjoy post-race pasta party and local villages like Beget and Oix.
Check weather for Pyrenean summer conditions; helmet mandatory.

videos.title: GRAN FONDO PIRINEOS: Puertos DUROS ⛰️y bajadas preciosas en la PRIMERA carrera en el canal! 🚵
videos.description:
Buena familia, 👋

este es el primer video de una carrera en la que he participado del CANAL! 

La hice hace un par de semanas en la zona norte de Cataluña pegando a los Pirineos, podréis ver que las vistas son increíbles y lo puertos duros.

Me lo pasé de maravilla y espero que a vosotros os guste también.

¿QUE PENSAIS? dejadlo en COMENTARIOS.

Si te ha gustado el video, deja tu LIKE y deja tu opinión en COMENTARIOS! 👍

No olvides SUSCRIBIRTE para no perderte nada de tu canal de ciclismo favorito!! No olvides activas las NOTIFICACIONES para no perderte el próximo video y, mientras tanto...

Instagram:  @call_of_cycling

¡¡Gracias por ver el video y bienvenido a la comunidad de CALL OF CYCLING!!💙🚴‍♂️

¡Un abrazo!

videos.title: Ride 443 - Day 4 of our epic ride through the Pyrenees. It got biblical
videos.description:
I joined a group of riders heading north from Girona to the Pyrenees. We planned to cross into France and then turn east towards the Mediterranean and then work our way along the Spanish (the Costa Brava) all the way back to Girona. I normally do not ride on consecutive days, and this challenge was spread across six consecutive days. I was really quite unsure whether I'd actually be able to complete it. Of course, that wasn't the story I told everyone else.

In this video, after leaving Camprodon, we have climbed north up the border between Spain and France. We descended down into France to the town of Prats de Mollo la Preste and stopped for lunch. It was pretty hot there, but we managed to get a table under cover. After a nice lunch and a beer or two, we started the portion of this day's ride, a descent east along the river through the canyon to Can Partere. We spent the rest of the afternoon lounging around the swimming pool enjoying the odd beverage. What an enjoyable day. Too bad we are expecting the weather to drastically change for tomorrow's stage. Oh well, that's then, and this is now. Cheers.

If you are interested in a cycling trip through the Pyrenees, check out Pyrenees cycling adventures:  https://pyrenees.cc/

videos.title: Ride 444 - Descent into Cadaquez, Spain. Former home to Salvador Dali.
videos.description:
I joined a group of riders heading north from Girona to the Pyrenees. We planned to cross into France and then turn east towards the Mediterranean and then work our way along the Spanish (the Costa Brava) all the way back to Girona. I normally do not ride on consecutive days, and this challenge was spread across six consecutive days. I was really quite unsure whether I'd actually be able to complete it. Of course, that wasn't the story I told everyone else.

In this video, after leaving Camprodon, we have climbed north up the border between Spain and France. We descended down into France to the town of Prats de Mollo la Preste and stopped for lunch. It was pretty hot there, but we managed to get a table under cover. After a nice lunch and a beer or two, we started the portion of this day's ride, a descent east along the river through the canyon to Can Partere. We spent the rest of the afternoon lounging around the swimming pool enjoying the odd beverage. What an enjoyable day. Too bad we are expecting the weather to drastically change for tomorrow's stage. Oh well, that's then, and this is now. Cheers.

If you are interested in a cycling trip through the Pyrenees, check out Pyrenees cycling adventures:  https://pyrenees.cc/

key_challenges.name: Hard right turn near hotel
key_challenges.description: Previous rider crashed here breaking five ribs and collarbone, warning to be careful on this turn

terrain_notes.text: Route is mainly downhill for the first half with all the climbing packed into the end

race_day_tips.text: Weather can change dramatically - riders warned about rain inundating the area and gray nasty skies replacing sun
Construction issues can force route diversions from planned course

additional_quotes.text: While we had planned to get out early and try our best to stay ahead of the expected storm, our start was delayed for several reasons. And now it's quite uncertain what weather we will encounter today.

rider_intel.search_text: The Gran Fondo Pirineus route through the Pyrenees features a distinct elevation profile with mainly downhill terrain for the first half and all climbing packed into the end. Riders report significant weather variability, with conditions changing from sunny to gray skies and rain threats that can affect start times. Construction issues may force route diversions from the planned course. The area has limited route options to reach the coast, often bringing riders back to familiar territory. Temperature was around 19C during one rider's experience. Weather forecasts consistently showed rain inundating the area, requiring early starts to stay ahead of storms. There's a notable dangerous hard right turn near the hotel finish area where crashes have occurred.
